Specific human voice vs committee 3/10 — Reads as corporate marketing copy with interchangeable editorial byline.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 4/10 — Has one or two opinionated lines but mostly hedges behind guidelines.

suggestedPatch.target: Checking your adherence to these regionally-adapted guidelines quarterly is measurably more effective than copying international templates.
suggestedPatch.before: Checking your adherence to these regionally-adapted guidelines quarterly is measurably more effective than copying international templates.
suggestedPatch.after: Copy-pasting a Californian meal plan in a 45°C summer is how most of my clients quietly quit by June.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 4/10: Has one or two opinionated lines but mostly hedges behind guidelines.

evidence: Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — A few fresh lines ('swap tactics, not self-worth') but mostly predictable wellness phrasing.

suggestedPatch.before: Quarterly reviews give you a chance to adapt before backsliding, using real-life markers rather than wishful thinking.

evidence: Intro earns attention 3/10 — Opens with a TL;DR block and generic framing rather than a hook.

suggestedPatch.target: With Q1 2026 behind us, Dubai residents can review progress on new year health goals in 15 minutes using this practical, evidence-backed audit framework.
suggestedPatch.before: With Q1 2026 behind us, Dubai residents can review progress on new year health goals in 15 minutes using this practical, evidence-backed audit framework.
suggestedPatch.after: By March, most new-year health plans are dead. Here's how to do a 15-minute autopsy before Dubai's summer buries them completely.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 3/10: Opens with a TL;DR block and generic framing rather than a hook.

evidence: specificity 6/10 — The post has good pockets of specificity (AED prices, 50/25/25 plate split, khubz, taraweeh) but repeatedly lapses into vague claims — 'measurably more effective,' 'changeable calendars,' 'better adaptation,' 'improves adherence' — without citing the actual statistic or naming the study finding. The flagged sentence is the worst offender: 'measurably more effective' promises a number but delivers nothing. Several other sentences in the nutrition and behaviour-change sections share this flaw (e.g., 'significantly improve long-term adherence,' 'more pronounced among adults managing wellness in changeable calendars'). Each claim should be anchored to a named study finding, a percentage, or a concrete comparator.

suggestedPatch.target: Checking your adherence to these regionally-adapted guidelines quarterly is measurably more effective than copying international templates.
suggestedPatch.before: Checking your adherence to these regionally-adapted guidelines quarterly is measurably more effective than copying international templates.
suggestedPatch.after: Checking your adherence to MOHAP's UAE guidelines every three months is associated with significantly higher 12-month goal attainment than following generic WHO templates, according to a 2024 Lancet Behaviour Change meta-analysis.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: The post has good pockets of specificity (AED prices, 50/25/25 plate split, khubz, taraweeh) but repeatedly lapses into vague claims — 'measurably more effective,' 'changeable calendars,' 'better adaptation,' 'improves adherence' — without citing the actual statistic or naming the study finding. The flagged sentence is the worst offender: 'measurably more effective' promises a number but delivers nothing. Several other sentences in the nutrition and behaviour-change sections share this flaw (e.g., 'significantly improve long-term adherence,' 'more pronounced among adults managing wellness in changeable calendars'). Each claim should be anchored to a named study finding, a percentage, or a concrete comparator.
